title: Traffic light controls are not keyboard-accessible

evidence.quote:
<div
                  key={color}
                  className="clickable relative rounded-full cursor-pointer mx-1 transition-all duration-300 hover:scale-105"

reasoning: The three state-changing lights are rendered as plain div elements with only an onClick handler. They are not focusable, have no button role or accessible name, and do not handle Enter/Space activation. Users navigating by keyboard or assistive technology cannot operate the widget even though changing the active light is the core interaction.
reproduction: Render the widget, press Tab, and observe that focus never lands on the red/orange/green light controls; pressing Enter or Space cannot change the active light.
recommendation: Render each light as a button with an accessible label, or add the equivalent role, tabIndex, aria-label, and keyboard handling for Enter and Space. Prefer real button elements with reset styling.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests are included for this feature, and there is no test asserting keyboard focusability or accessible activation of the light controls.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a React Testing Library test that tabs to each light button, activates it with Enter or Space, and asserts the status message updates for red, orange, and green.
minimumFixScope: Update the mapped light control element in packages/teacher/src/features/widgets/trafficLight/trafficLight.tsx and add a focused component test.
